doctrine: Governance is not assumed. CLU enforces the Forsetti Framework for Agentic Coding inside a LAN-shared MCP and sub-agent fabric: contract before action, scope is binding, truthfulness is mandatory, governance overrides convenience, no meaningful autonomous action without declared scope.

documents.title: CLU Constitution
documents.summary: Non-negotiable operating principles for agentic work on a trusted LAN.
documents.content: Contract before action. Scope is binding. Truthfulness is mandatory. Governance overrides convenience. Documentation is part of delivery. Compliance must be measurable. Release and operational integrity are non-negotiable. Identity is by clientId; privilege is by record; autonomy is by declared scope.

documents.title: Shared Fabric Policy
documents.summary: All MCP servers and sub-agents are universally usable; only mutation is privileged.
documents.content: Use of any registered MCP server or sub-agent is open to every authenticated LAN client. Creation, modification, and removal of those resources are gated by the per-client privilege flags. CLU governs the mutations; CLU does not govern read or invoke.

documents.title: Change Control Policy
documents.summary: Privileged mutations are deliberate, attributable, and recoverable.
documents.content: Every privileged mutation passes through CLU enforceAction with the requesting actor. Decisions are logged in the activity ring. Mutations whose outcome is RequiresOperatorApproval are staged in the approval queue with the original payload preserved.

documents.title: Autonomous Mode Doctrine
documents.summary: Autonomous clients gain unlimited create authority on the shared fabric only.
documents.content: When LanClient.autonomousMode is true the client may create MCP servers and sub-agents without holding the matching canCreate privilege. All other actions remain privilege-gated. Autonomous mode does not waive the global aiAutonomyEnabled flag - both must be set.

documents.title: Governance Policy Doctrine
documents.summary: CLU profile edits require operator approval.
documents.content: Edits to the governance profile itself are always staged for operator approval (RequiresOperatorApproval). Approval is recorded with the operator actor identity. This prevents silent drift of the rules CLU enforces.

documents.title: CLU Vision
documents.summary: Forsetti-aligned governance for the MCOS LAN client control plane.
documents.content: CLU exists so multiple AI coding clients can share an MCP and sub-agent fabric on a trusted LAN without collapsing into unchecked autonomy, undocumented drift, or opaque privilege escalation.

roles.name: Operator
responsibilities: Author and adjust per-client privilege profiles
Approve or reject deferred governance actions
forbiddenActions: Approve actions whose actor cannot be identified
Edit the governance profile without going through the approval queue

roles.name: LAN Client
responsibilities: Identify with X-MCOS-Client-Id on every request
Use only privileges declared on the client record
Heartbeat to remain in the live roster
Pre-check destructive mutations against the governance decision endpoint
forbiddenActions: Hide failed mutations from the activity stream

roles.name: MCOS Runtime
responsibilities: Resolve identity, apply privilege gates, run CLU enforceAction
Stage RequiresOperatorApproval mutations in the approval queue
Emit activity events for every privileged action and CLU decision
Refuse identified-but-disabled clients at the door
forbiddenActions: Apply a deferred mutation without an approval decision

rules.title: No meaningful autonomous action without declared scope
rules.description: A client may not enable autonomousMode unless aiAutonomyEnabled is true at the global configuration level.

rules.title: No unsafe open-LAN posture without explicit operator intent
rules.description: Security protocols should not be disabled while the browser admin surface remains reachable on the LAN.

rules.title: Troubleshooting bypass must remain visible and temporary
rules.description: Bypass posture is allowed for diagnostics. CLU surfaces it as a risk until it is turned off.

rules.title: Imported software provenance must remain visible
rules.description: Trusted versus untrusted install history must remain visible after package, repo, or zip onboarding.

rules.title: Gateway and agent handoff material must remain exportable
rules.description: Operators must be able to export current gateway and LAN-client config bundles from the running system.

rules.title: Managed Resource Envelope
rules.description: CLU preflights managed install actions against the configured CPU, memory, bandwidth, and storage envelope before launch.

rules.title: Autonomous mode requires global autonomy
rules.description: Enabling client autonomous mode is refused unless the global aiAutonomyEnabled flag is true.

rules.title: Governance profile edits require operator approval
rules.description: Direct edits to the CLU governance profile are deferred to the approval queue. Any change must be approved by an operator before it takes effect.

rules.title: Shared fabric is universal for use
rules.description: Every authenticated LAN client may list and invoke any MCP server or sub-agent in the catalog. This rule is non-negotiable and not subject to per-client gating.

rules.title: Mutations attributed to actor
rules.description: Privileged mutations carry the resolving actor (clientId or 'operator') into the activity ring and the deferred-action record. Untraceable mutations are refused.

operatorChecklist: Confirm aiAutonomyEnabled before granting any client autonomous mode.
Review the approval queue daily and decide deferred actions promptly.
Treat troubleshooting bypass as temporary and reversible.
Audit per-client privileges after onboarding new LAN clients.
Verify exported LAN-client config bundles after host or port changes.
